description: Craig Whittaker was Treasurer of HM Household (Deputy Chief Whip) between 7 September 2022 and 27 October 2022. He was previously Government Whip, Lord Commissioner of HM Treasury between 8 July 2022 to 7 September 2022 and Vice Chamberlain of Her Majesty’s Household (Government Whip) between 16 April 2019 and 28 July 2019. Craig was Government Whip (Lord Commissioner of HM Treasury) from January 2018 to April 2019 and an Assistant Government Whip in the House of Commons from June 2017 to January 2018. He was first elected to Parliament as MP for Calder Valley in 2010. Political career Craig served as the Private Parliamentary Secretary to James Brokenshire when he was Minister of State for Immigration. He was a member of the Education Select Committee from 2010 to 2015.

details.body:
Craig Whittaker was Treasurer of HM Household (Deputy Chief Whip) between 7 September 2022 and 27 October 2022.

He was previously Government Whip, Lord Commissioner of HM Treasury between 8 July 2022 to 7 September 2022 and Vice Chamberlain of Her Majesty’s Household (Government Whip) between 16 April 2019 and 28 July 2019.

Craig was Government Whip (Lord Commissioner of HM Treasury) from January 2018 to April 2019 and an Assistant Government Whip in the House of Commons from June 2017 to January 2018.

He was first elected to Parliament as MP for Calder Valley in 2010.

Political career

Craig served as the Private Parliamentary Secretary to James Brokenshire when he was Minister of State for Immigration. He was a member of the Education Select Committee from 2010 to 2015.

Craig Whittaker was Treasurer of HM Household (Deputy Chief Whip) between 7 September 2022 and 27 October 2022.

He was previously Government Whip, Lord Commissioner of HM Treasury between 8 July 2022 to 7 September 2022 and Vice Chamberlain of Her Majesty’s Household (Government Whip) between 16 April 2019 and 28 July 2019.

Craig was Government Whip (Lord Commissioner of HM Treasury) from January 2018 to April 2019 and an Assistant Government Whip in the House of Commons from June 2017 to January 2018.

He was first elected to Parliament as MP for Calder Valley in 2010.

Political career

Craig served as the Private Parliamentary Secretary to James Brokenshire when he was Minister of State for Immigration. He was a member of the Education Select Committee from 2010 to 2015.

Whips are MPs or Lords appointed by each party to help organise their party’s contribution to parliamentary business. They are responsible for making sure the maximum number of their party members vote, and vote the way their party wants.
